meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Solve Debugging Issues with Project Management
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn how systematic project management and AI-driven tools like Antigravity and Claude Code can accelerate your development journey.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about reading code; it's about managing complexity. By adopting a structured project management workflow, you isolate errors, track state changes, and use AI agents to pinpoint logic failures. Use Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for automated execution to turn roadblocks into learning milestones.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, the clock is ticking, and your code is throwing an error you haven't seen before. That sinking feeling of being stuck isn't a failure—it's the friction of growth. Every senior engineer has stared at a cryptic stack trace in the dead of night, wondering if they chose the right path.

But here is the secret: debugging is 20% syntax and 80% project management. When you stop treating your app like a chaotic file dump and start treating it like a managed system, those 'unsolvable' bugs become clear technical debt. You are about to transform from a student struggling with errors into a builder who masters their environment.

steps.title: Define the Scope of the Bug
steps.description: Before fixing, isolate the failing module. Document the expected vs. actual behavior to create a clear objective for the AI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and identify which module is causing the 'undefined' error during runtime. List the dependencies that might be failing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ls --files && claude ask 'Summarize the logic flow of the current module.'

steps.title: Implement Defensive Logging
steps.description: Inject structured logging to visualize data flow, allowing the AI to see exactly where the state deviates from expectations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest a logging strategy for this function that tracks input variables and return states for debugging purposes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add console logs to track the state of the user object before the API call.'

steps.title: Automate Traceability
steps.description: Use Claude Code to run tests against the specific function, creating a repeatable loop for debugging.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a unit test scenario that reproduces the current bug consistently.

steps.title: Refactor with AI Oversight
steps.description: Apply the fix while ensuring no side effects are introduced, maintaining the integrity of your project management board.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for the identified bug that adheres to clean code principles and integrates with the existing architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Refactor the handleLogin function to include error handling for null responses.'

steps.title: Document the Fix
steps.description: Update your project documentation so you never face this specific error again, turning the bug into a lesson.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a brief technical post-mortem summarizing the bug, the fix, and the preventative measure for my project wiki.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Append a debugging note to README.md regarding the handling of API timeouts.'

pro_tips: Always commit your code before triggering an AI refactor so you can revert if the logic becomes too abstract.
Treat every bug as a feature request for better error handling; don't just patch, build resilience.
Use Claude Code to explain complex stack traces in plain English before asking for a solution.
Keep your project file structure flat; nested complexity is the primary hiding place for elusive bugs.
Practice 'Rubber Ducking' with your AI agent; explaining the bug to the AI often reveals the solution before you even send the prompt.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your current project, deploy it to a staging environment to ensure the fix holds under real-world conditions.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop fearing bugs and start mastering your codebase. Apply these patterns to your next project to see your velocity increase exponentially. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
